doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_get_cputime (mpfr/src/logging.c L114-L132). Returns elapsed user-mode CPU time in milliseconds as an int. The C implementation uses getrusage on POSIX or clock() on ANSI-only systems. In libmpfr, this function exists for logging instrumentation only; it is part of the MPFR_USE_LOGGING-conditional compilation block.

The TS port returns 0 unconditionally. CPU time is not portably observable from pure-TS in a way that matches the C semantics (Bun and Node have process.cpuUsage().user but it's microseconds and TS callers should use performance.now() for wall time anyway). The C function exists for logging instrumentation, not for application logic; the TS port is a no-op that documents the gap. The golden_driver emits 0 unconditionally to match.

Tag minimums per Rule 7 inapplicable; single happy case (carve-out per no-arg accessor pattern).
divergence_from_c:
Two divergences:

  1. Return value: C returns elapsed user-mode CPU ms since process start; TS returns 0 unconditionally. The C function is for libmpfr internal logging only -- this is not load-bearing for any other port.

  2. Return type: C returns int; TS returns number (consistent with TS-native numeric scalar return convention).

The golden_driver emits 0 to match the TS contract; calling the actual mpfr_get_cputime would yield a host-dependent observation that varies per invocation, making the golden non-reproducible.
refs: mpfr/src/logging.c L114-L132 -- the C reference body.
CLAUDE.md Rule 12 -- pure-TS port; no Bun-specific or Node-specific APIs in src/.
eval/functions/mpfr_buildopt_float128_p/spec.json -- precedent: divergent behavior whose golden hard-codes the TS-expected value.
